channel.title: Health - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.title: Psychology says the loneliest thing about getting older isn't what you lose, it's the growing recognition that most of the people you love are loving a version of you that's five, ten, or twenty years out of date - VegOut
item.description:
1. Psychology says the loneliest thing about getting older isn't what you lose, it's the growing recognition that most of the people you love are loving a version of you that's five, ten, or twenty years out of date  VegOut
  2. Psychologists explain that the people who feel most alone at family gatherings aren't disconnected from their families — they've simply outgrown the version of themselves everyone there still expects them to perform  Space Daily
  3. Psychology tells us that people who feel lonely even when surrounded by others aren’t exaggerating or being ungrateful; they often experience a more subtle form of disconnection in which no one seems to connect with their true self  OkDiario
  4. Once you’re over 65, one of the most isolating realizations is that the people who love you are actually in love with a version of you that’s 20 years out of date  Yahoo
  5. There's a specific kind of loneliness that only happens in rooms full of people who know a version of you that expired years ago  VegOut
